 {"id":"bd-1dt84","title":"Feature: Budget & Resource Control - Token caps and early stopping","description":"# Feature: Budget & Resource Control

**Critical for preventing token black holes.** Ensemble runs can be expensive - this feature provides predictability and control.

## Budget Configuration

### BudgetConfig (consistent with types.go)
```yaml
budget:
  per_agent: 4000      # Max tokens per mode agent
  total: 50000         # Max for entire ensemble run
  synthesis: 8000      # Reserved for synthesis phase
  context_pack: 2000   # Reserved for context injection
```

### Early Stopping (Marginal Utility)
Stop when new findings rate drops below threshold:
```go
type EarlyStopConfig struct {
    MinAgentsBeforeStop int     // Run at least N agents
    FindingsThreshold   float64 // Stop if new findings/token < threshold
    SimilarityThreshold float64 // Stop if output similarity > threshold
    WindowSize          int     // Evaluate over last N agents
}
```

### Context Pack Caching
- Cache generated context packs
- Reuse across ensemble runs
- Invalidate on code changes or explicit request
- Track cache hit rate in metrics

## Real-Time Monitoring

### Token Velocity Dashboard
```
Budget: 23,456 / 50,000 tokens (47%)
████████████░░░░░░░░░░░░░░

Agent Spend:
[0] F1 Argument Mapping    3,847 tokens  ✓
[1] E2 Root Cause          4,012 tokens  ✓ (cap hit)
[2] K1 Systems Thinking    2,891 tokens  ◐
[3] L3 Meta-cognitive      pending
[4] H2 Strategic           pending

Findings Rate: 2.3 findings/1k tokens
Early Stop: disabled (4 more agents needed)
```

## Alerts & Guardrails
- Warn at 80% budget consumed
- Auto-stop at 100% with partial synthesis
- Log all cap hits for tuning
- Suggest budget adjustments based on history

## Files
- internal/ensemble/budget.go - Budget types and tracking
- internal/ensemble/early_stop.go - Marginal utility detection
- internal/ensemble/metrics.go - Findings velocity, similarity
- internal/tui/budget_panel.go - Dashboard component","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:57:48.239196966Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T01:50:26.586537664Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T01:50:26.586517856Z","close_reason":"Core Budget & Resource Control implemented: BudgetTracker (budget.go) with per-agent/total tracking and enforcement, EarlyStopDetector (early_stop.go) with findings rate and similarity thresholds, token estimation via internal/tokens, ContextPackCache (context_cache.go) with TTL and invalidation. All components have comprehensive unit tests and integration tests. EarlyStop is wired into pipeline stage2. TUI dashboard (metrics_panel.go) and detailed metrics are scoped to bd-397f0 (Observability Metrics).","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dt84","depends_on_id":"bd-12u4u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:24Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1dt84","depends_on_id":"bd-49fb9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:24Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1ed7","title":"E2E Tests: Full session recovery workflow","description":"## End-to-End Test Suite for Session Recovery

### E2E Test Scenarios
1. **Clean session save/restore**
   - Spawn session with 3 agents
   - Execute some commands
   - Kill session (simulate crash)
   - Run ntm recover
   - Verify all panes restored with scrollback

2. **Partial recovery**
   - Spawn session
   - Corrupt one pane's state file
   - Recover
   - Verify remaining panes restored
   - Verify error reported for corrupted pane

3. **Recovery after long idle**
   - Spawn session
   - Wait 30+ seconds
   - Kill and recover
   - Verify state still valid

4. **Multi-project recovery**
   - Spawn sessions for 2 projects
   - Kill both
   - Recover each independently

### Detailed Logging
log.Printf("[E2E-RECOVERY] Spawning session: %d panes", numPanes)
log.Printf("[E2E-RECOVERY] State captured: %d bytes", stateSize)
log.Printf("[E2E-RECOVERY] Simulating crash...")
log.Printf("[E2E-RECOVERY] Restoring pane %d/%d: %s", i, total, paneName)
log.Printf("[E2E-RECOVERY] Scrollback match: %v (%d lines)", match, lines)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:12:50.783368496Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T16:34:19.380086093Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T16:34:19.380006103Z","close_reason":"E2E test suite implemented with 5 comprehensive tests: TestFullSessionRecovery (full spawn/crash/recover cycle), TestPartialRecovery (corruption handling), TestRecoveryAfterLongIdle (idle period resilience), TestMultiProjectRecovery (cross-project recovery), TestRecoveryContextBuilding (context validation). All tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ed7","depends_on_id":"bd-38kx","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:24Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ed7","depends_on_id":"bd-3law","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:24Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1es69","title":"process_triage: Implement background health monitoring loop","description":"Create background goroutine that monitors agent health via pt.

## Requirements

1. Monitor all agent panes continuously
2. Call pt with agent PIDs at configured interval
3. Track classification history
4. Trigger alerts on threshold breach

## Implementation

```go
package pt

type HealthMonitor struct {
    config      *config.ProcessTriageConfig
    pidMap      *rano.PIDMap  // reuse from rano integration
    states      map[string]*AgentState  // pane -> state
    alertCh     chan Alert
    stopCh      chan struct{}
}

type AgentState struct {
    Pane           string
    Classification string  // useful, waiting, idle, stuck, zombie
    Since          time.Time
    History        []ClassificationEvent
}

func (m *HealthMonitor) Start() {
    go m.monitorLoop()
}

func (m *HealthMonitor) monitorLoop() {
    ticker := time.NewTicker(time.Duration(m.config.CheckInterval) * time.Second)
    defer ticker.Stop()
    
    for {
        select {
        case <-ticker.C:
            m.checkAll()
        case <-m.stopCh:
            return
        }
    }
}

func (m *HealthMonitor) checkAll() {
    for pane, pid := range m.pidMap.GetAll() {
        classification := m.classify(pid)
        m.updateState(pane, classification)
        m.checkAlerts(pane)
    }
}
```

## Code Location
- internal/integrations/pt/monitor.go

## Integration with rano
If rano enabled, include network activity in classification:
- Network activity → likely waiting, not stuck","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:21:56.937143241Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T01:42:55.094897305Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T01:42:55.094809931Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1es69","depends_on_id":"bd-1h5xe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:24Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1es69","depends_on_id":"bd-1sa5u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:24Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1es69","depends_on_id":"bd-27l8q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:24Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1es69","depends_on_id":"bd-lzvh2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:24Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-in4ac","title":"Feature: Prompt Preflight (lint + preview + policy)","description":"## Summary
Add a preflight step for outbound prompts (send/palette/workflows) that:
- shows exactly what will be sent (after template expansion / file injection)
- runs lightweight lint rules (oversized prompts, missing context, secrets/PII)
- surfaces policy risk signals (destructive instructions) in a consistent, testable way

## Why
Prompt mistakes are costly:
- wasted tokens/time across many agents
- accidental leakage of secrets
- accidental instructions for destructive actions

## UX
- New command: `ntm prompt preflight <session> --cc|--cod|--gmi --message ...` (name TBD)
- Flag on send/palette: `--preflight` or `--no-preflight` (if we default it on later)

## Output (human)
- Token estimate
- Target panes
- Lint findings (severity)
- Final prompt preview (optionally truncated)

## Output (robot)
- `success` bool
- `findings[]` with stable fields + severities
- `preview_hash` + preview length
- Stable `error_code` when blocking

## Integrations
- Secrets/PII detection must reuse the unified redaction engine.
- Destructive-instruction evaluation should integrate with DCG (`bd-31r17`) when available, and degrade gracefully when not.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Works for both raw send and palette-driven prompts.
- Unit tests for core lint rules.
- E2E test that preflight catches a synthetic secret and a dangerously destructive prompt.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:25:21.007585818Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:53:14.737486601Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-in4ac","depends_on_id":"bd-27hn0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:25:21.021371326Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf"}]}

 {"id":"bd-j44fa","title":"Task: Implement handoff writer module","description":"## Overview
Implement the handoff writer module that serializes Handoff structs to YAML files in the correct directory structure with atomic writes and comprehensive logging.

## Background & Reasoning
Handoffs must be stored in a predictable location so that:
1. Session-start can find the most recent handoff
2. Status line can extract goal/now fields
3. Multiple sessions can coexist without conflicts
4. Files are human-readable for debugging
5. Concurrent writes don't corrupt data

## Directory Structure
```
.ntm/
├── handoffs/
│   ├── {session-name}/
│   │   ├── 2026-01-19_14-30_auth-complete.yaml
│   │   ├── 2026-01-19_16-00_tests-passing.yaml
│   │   ├── auto-handoff-2026-01-19T14-30-00.yaml
│   │   └── .archive/  # Old handoffs moved here after rotation
│   │       └── 2026-01-18_10-00_old-handoff.yaml
│   └── general/
└── ledgers/
```

## Deliverables

### 1. Create internal/handoff/writer.go
```go
package handoff

import (
    "fmt"
    "log/slog"
    "os"
    "path/filepath"
    "regexp"
    "strings"
    "sync"
    "time"

    "gopkg.in/yaml.v3"
)

var (
    // Mutex for concurrent write protection
    writeMu sync.Mutex

    // Valid session name pattern
    sessionNameRegex = regexp.MustCompile(`^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$`)

    // Valid description pattern (kebab-case)
    descriptionRegex = regexp.MustCompile(`^[a-z0-9-]+$`)
)

// Writer handles writing handoff files to disk
type Writer struct {
    baseDir    string // typically .ntm/handoffs
    maxPerDir  int    // max handoffs before rotation (default 50)
    logger     *slog.Logger
}

// NewWriter creates a Writer for the given project directory
func NewWriter(projectDir string) *Writer {
    return &Writer{
        baseDir:   filepath.Join(projectDir, ".ntm", "handoffs"),
        maxPerDir: 50,
        logger:    slog.Default().With("component", "handoff.writer"),
    }
}

// Write saves a handoff to the appropriate directory using atomic write
// Returns the path to the written file
func (w *Writer) Write(h *Handoff, description string) (string, error) {
    writeMu.Lock()
    defer writeMu.Unlock()

    w.logger.Debug("starting handoff write",
        "session", h.Session,
        "description", description,
        "goal_length", len(h.Goal),
        "now_length", len(h.Now),
    )

    // Validate handoff
    if errs := h.Validate(); len(errs) > 0 {
        w.logger.Error("handoff validation failed",
            "session", h.Session,
            "error_count", len(errs),
            "first_error", errs[0].Error(),
        )
        return "", fmt.Errorf("validation failed: %v", errs[0])
    }

    // Validate session name
    if h.Session != "general" && !sessionNameRegex.MatchString(h.Session) {
        w.logger.Error("invalid session name",
            "session", h.Session,
        )
        return "", fmt.Errorf("invalid session name: %s", h.Session)
    }

    // Sanitize description
    desc := sanitizeDescription(description)
    if desc == "" {
        desc = "handoff"
    }

    // Ensure directory exists
    if err := w.EnsureDir(h.Session); err != nil {
        return "", err
    }

    // Check for rotation
    if err := w.checkRotation(h.Session); err != nil {
        w.logger.Warn("rotation check failed", "error", err)
        // Non-fatal, continue with write
    }

    // Set defaults
    h.SetDefaults()

    // Generate filename
    filename := fmt.Sprintf("%s_%s.yaml",
        time.Now().Format("2006-01-02_15-04"),
        desc,
    )
    path := filepath.Join(w.baseDir, h.Session, filename)

    // Serialize to YAML
    data, err := yaml.Marshal(h)
    if err != nil {
        w.logger.Error("yaml marshaling failed",
            "session", h.Session,
            "error", err,
        )
        return "", fmt.Errorf("marshal failed: %w", err)
    }

    // Atomic write: write to temp, then rename
    if err := w.atomicWrite(path, data); err != nil {
        return "", err
    }

    w.logger.Info("handoff written successfully",
        "path", path,
        "session", h.Session,
        "goal", truncateLog(h.Goal, 50),
        "now", truncateLog(h.Now, 50),
        "size_bytes", len(data),
    )

    return path, nil
}

// WriteAuto saves an auto-generated handoff with timestamp naming
func (w *Writer) WriteAuto(h *Handoff) (string, error) {
    writeMu.Lock()
    defer writeMu.Unlock()

    w.logger.Debug("starting auto-handoff write",
        "session", h.Session,
        "tokens_pct", h.TokensPct,
    )

    // Validate
    if errs := h.Validate(); len(errs) > 0 {
        w.logger.Error("auto-handoff validation failed",
            "session", h.Session,
            "error_count", len(errs),
        )
        return "", fmt.Errorf("validation failed: %v", errs[0])
    }

    // Ensure directory
    if err := w.EnsureDir(h.Session); err != nil {
        return "", err
    }

    // Set defaults
    h.SetDefaults()

    // Generate auto filename with ISO8601 timestamp
    filename := fmt.Sprintf("auto-handoff-%s.yaml",
        time.Now().Format("2006-01-02T15-04-05"),
    )
    path := filepath.Join(w.baseDir, h.Session, filename)

    // Serialize
    data, err := yaml.Marshal(h)
    if err != nil {
        return "", fmt.Errorf("marshal failed: %w", err)
    }

    // Atomic write
    if err := w.atomicWrite(path, data); err != nil {
        return "", err
    }

    w.logger.Info("auto-handoff written",
        "path", path,
        "session", h.Session,
        "tokens_pct", h.TokensPct,
        "size_bytes", len(data),
    )

    return path, nil
}

// EnsureDir creates the handoff directory for a session if needed
func (w *Writer) EnsureDir(sessionName string) error {
    dir := filepath.Join(w.baseDir, sessionName)

    if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0755); err != nil {
        w.logger.Error("failed to create handoff directory",
            "dir", dir,
            "error", err,
        )
        return fmt.Errorf("mkdir failed: %w", err)
    }

    w.logger.Debug("ensured handoff directory", "dir", dir)
    return nil
}

// atomicWrite writes data to a temp file then renames to target
func (w *Writer) atomicWrite(path string, data []byte) error {
    dir := filepath.Dir(path)

    // Create temp file in same directory (ensures same filesystem)
    tmp, err := os.CreateTemp(dir, ".handoff-*.tmp")
    if err != nil {
        w.logger.Error("failed to create temp file",
            "dir", dir,
            "error", err,
        )
        return fmt.Errorf("temp file creation failed: %w", err)
    }
    tmpPath := tmp.Name()

    // Cleanup on failure
    defer func() {
        if tmpPath != "" {
            os.Remove(tmpPath)
        }
    }()

    // Write data
    if _, err := tmp.Write(data); err != nil {
        tmp.Close()
        w.logger.Error("failed to write temp file",
            "path", tmpPath,
            "error", err,
        )
        return fmt.Errorf("write failed: %w", err)
    }

    // Sync to disk
    if err := tmp.Sync(); err != nil {
        tmp.Close()
        w.logger.Error("failed to sync temp file",
            "path", tmpPath,
            "error", err,
        )
        return fmt.Errorf("sync failed: %w", err)
    }

    // Close before rename
    if err := tmp.Close(); err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("close failed: %w", err)
    }

    // Set permissions
    if err := os.Chmod(tmpPath, 0644); err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("chmod failed: %w", err)
    }

    // Atomic rename
    if err := os.Rename(tmpPath, path); err != nil {
        w.logger.Error("failed to rename temp to target",
            "temp", tmpPath,
            "target", path,
            "error", err,
        )
        return fmt.Errorf("rename failed: %w", err)
    }

    tmpPath = "" // Prevent cleanup of successful write
    return nil
}

// checkRotation moves old handoffs to .archive if count exceeds max
func (w *Writer) checkRotation(sessionName string) error {
    dir := filepath.Join(w.baseDir, sessionName)
    archiveDir := filepath.Join(dir, ".archive")

    entries, err := os.ReadDir(dir)
    if err != nil {
        return err
    }

    // Count YAML files (exclude .archive dir)
    var yamlFiles []os.DirEntry
    for _, e := range entries {
        if !e.IsDir() && strings.HasSuffix(e.Name(), ".yaml") {
            yamlFiles = append(yamlFiles, e)
        }
    }

    if len(yamlFiles) <= w.maxPerDir {
        return nil // No rotation needed
    }

    // Create archive dir
    if err := os.MkdirAll(archiveDir, 0755); err != nil {
        return err
    }

    // Sort by name (which includes timestamp) - oldest first
    // Move oldest files to archive
    toMove := len(yamlFiles) - w.maxPerDir
    for i := 0; i < toMove; i++ {
        oldPath := filepath.Join(dir, yamlFiles[i].Name())
        newPath := filepath.Join(archiveDir, yamlFiles[i].Name())
        if err := os.Rename(oldPath, newPath); err != nil {
            w.logger.Warn("failed to archive handoff",
                "path", oldPath,
                "error", err,
            )
        } else {
            w.logger.Debug("archived old handoff",
                "from", oldPath,
                "to", newPath,
            )
        }
    }

    w.logger.Info("handoff rotation completed",
        "session", sessionName,
        "archived", toMove,
    )

    return nil
}

// sanitizeDescription converts to kebab-case and limits length
func sanitizeDescription(desc string) string {
    // Lowercase
    desc = strings.ToLower(desc)
    // Replace spaces/underscores with hyphens
    desc = strings.ReplaceAll(desc, " ", "-")
    desc = strings.ReplaceAll(desc, "_", "-")
    // Remove non-alphanumeric except hyphens
    var result strings.Builder
    for _, r := range desc {
        if (r >= 'a' && r <= 'z') || (r >= '0' && r <= '9') || r == '-' {
            result.WriteRune(r)
        }
    }
    desc = result.String()
    // Collapse multiple hyphens
    for strings.Contains(desc, "--") {
        desc = strings.ReplaceAll(desc, "--", "-")
    }
    // Trim hyphens from ends
    desc = strings.Trim(desc, "-")
    // Limit length
    if len(desc) > 50 {
        desc = desc[:50]
    }
    return desc
}

// truncateLog truncates string for logging
func truncateLog(s string, max int) string {
    if len(s) <= max {
        return s
    }
    return s[:max] + "..."
}
```

### 2. File Naming Convention
- Manual: `YYYY-MM-DD_HH-MM_{description}.yaml`
- Auto: `auto-handoff-{ISO8601-timestamp}.yaml`
- Description: kebab-case, max 50 chars, sanitized

### 3. YAML Output Format
```yaml
---
version: "1.0"
session: myproject
date: 2026-01-19
created_at: 2026-01-19T14:30:00Z
updated_at: 2026-01-19T14:30:00Z
status: complete
outcome: SUCCEEDED
---

goal: Implemented JWT authentication
now: Add refresh token support
test: go test ./internal/auth/...

done_this_session:
  - task: Created JWT token generation
    files: [internal/auth/jwt.go]
  - task: Added auth middleware
    files: [internal/middleware/auth.go]

# ... rest of fields
```

### 4. Rotation Policy
- Default: Keep 50 most recent handoffs per session
- Older handoffs moved to `.archive/` subdirectory
- Archive provides audit trail without cluttering main directory
- Configurable via Writer.maxPerDir

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Writer creates session directories as needed (0755 permissions)
- [ ] Manual handoffs use descriptive file names with sanitization
- [ ] Auto handoffs use ISO8601 timestamp file names
- [ ] YAML output matches exact format spec with frontmatter
- [ ] File permissions are 0644
- [ ] Atomic writes: temp file + rename pattern
- [ ] Concurrent writes protected by mutex
- [ ] Rotation moves old files to .archive when > 50
- [ ] All operations logged with structured slog
- [ ] Validation runs before write (returns error on invalid)
- [ ] Invalid session names rejected with clear error
- [ ] Unit tests for all writer methods
- [ ] Unit tests for concurrent write safety
- [ ] Unit tests for rotation logic

## Technical Notes
- Use yaml.Marshal for serialization
- Use sync.Mutex for write concurrency protection
- Atomic write pattern: CreateTemp → Write → Sync → Close → Chmod → Rename
- SetDefaults called before write to ensure timestamps set
- Validation must pass before any write attempt

## Logging Requirements
- DEBUG: Write operation start with session, description, goal/now lengths
- DEBUG: Directory creation
- DEBUG: Archived handoff paths
- INFO: Successful write with path, session, truncated goal/now, size
- INFO: Rotation completed with count
- WARN: Rotation failures (non-fatal)
- ERROR: Validation failures with error count
- ERROR: Filesystem operation failures with specific error","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:49:36.071375754Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T02:44:08.469029500Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T02:44:08.468964248Z","close_reason":"Implemented handoff writer module with atomic writes, rotation policy, and comprehensive tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-j44fa","depends_on_id":"bd-4gaaz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:30Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-j44fa","depends_on_id":"bd-khux0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:30Z","created_by":"import"}]}
